purpose: CLI entry-point for inspecting and upgrading the dev environment; provides report, check, doctor, outdated, and upgrade subcommands backed by manifest/tools.json.
invariants: Self-locating: resolves REPO_ROOT from the script's real path so symlink invocation works correctly
All subcommands except upgrade are read-only and never modify the system
cmd_check exits non-zero if any manifest tool binary is absent
upgrade does not modify manifest/tools.json — it only upgrades installed software
PATH is augmented at startup to include ~/.local/bin, ~/tools/bin, ~/.cargo/bin
gotchas: cmd_outdated queries PyPI and GitHub APIs over the network — may be slow or fail without internet
apt outdated detection falls back to dpkg -S when the manifest name differs from the apt package name
cmd_upgrade for github-zip/github-deb tools only prints a warning — it cannot auto-upgrade them; bootstrap.sh must be rerun
compat_requires warnings are advisory only; no enforcement is done during upgrade

key_functions.name: cmd_report
key_functions.summary: Prints a grouped, human-readable inventory of all tools from the manifest

key_functions.name: cmd_check
key_functions.summary: Verifies every manifest tool binary is present on PATH; exits 1 on drift

key_functions.name: cmd_doctor
key_functions.summary: Checks PATH, shellrc wiring, rust env, docker daemon, version stamp, and manifest drift; the /mnt/* filesystem check is only performed on WSL (detected via /proc/version)

key_functions.name: cmd_outdated
key_functions.summary: Queries apt, PyPI, GitHub API, rustup, and npm for newer versions and prints a per-tool diff

key_functions.name: cmd_upgrade
key_functions.summary: Upgrades all tools (or a named one) via their respective install method with compat warnings
